title: artocarpus humilis
intro: Artocarpus humilis, commonly known as the tamarillo or tree tomato, is a species of flowering plant in the mulberry family, native to the Andes mountain range. The plant is known for its edible red or yellow fruits, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to treat various health issues such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Artocarpus humilis include its ability to boost the immune system, lower blood pressure, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help to protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases. Artocarpus humilis can be prepared as a herbal tea, juice, or powder, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
